subject: Psychology
grade_subject_fit: Source-derived Cycle A biopsychology package aligned to the master spec: teacher/student separation, evidence tasks, safe fictional cases, decision plus reason, and tiered major work.
unit_context: Cycle A A2 introduces brain, nervous system, endocrine system, and behaviour without reducing people to brain-only explanations.
assignment_purpose: Students explain behaviour using biological systems while preserving context, evidence, and limits.
final_evidence_target: A fictional case explanation that connects brain/body systems to behaviour, names evidence, includes an alternative, and avoids diagnosis.

student_task_flow.label: Core concepts
student_task_flow.evidence: Students define neuron, neurotransmitter, nervous system, endocrine system, brain region, stress response, evidence, limit.

student_task_flow.label: Fictional case set
student_task_flow.evidence: Students use evidence from general cases to explain behaviour with biology and context.

student_task_flow.label: Decision plus reason
student_task_flow.evidence: Students choose the strongest explanation and justify it with evidence and limits.

success_criteria: I can describe how brain and body systems influence behaviour.
I can use evidence from a fictional case.
I can make a decision and give a reason.
I can include a limit or alternative explanation.
differentiation_model: Supported students use a labelled system map. Proficient students choose the best biological explanation. Extending students compare biological and contextual explanations and evaluate what evidence would strengthen each.
checkpoint_release_logic: Students complete vocabulary and system map before writing the final case explanation.
teacher_implementation_notes: Use fictional or general examples only.
Do not ask students to disclose medical, medication, or family history.
Require evidence, context, and limits in major responses.
likely_misconceptions: A brain region label is a full explanation.
Stress is only emotional rather than body and context together.
pacing_shape: Five-part source-derived package: vocabulary, brain/body systems, case evidence, explanation comparison, final case analysis.

teacher_guide.title: Cycle A A2 Teacher Guide — Brain, Body, and Behaviour
teacher_guide.overview: Students learn that behaviour is influenced by brain and body systems, but responsible psychology explanations also include context and limits.
teacher_guide.essential_question: How do brain and body systems influence behaviour without explaining everything by themselves?
teacher_guide.big_idea: Biological explanations are strongest when they connect systems to evidence while avoiding reductionism.
teacher_guide.opening_frame: Tell students that biology matters, but a careful psychology explanation does not turn one brain term into a whole-person label.
teacher_guide.frame_line: A2 frame: system, evidence, context, limit.
teacher_guide.assessment_focus: Final case explanation should connect one or more biological systems to behaviour, cite evidence, include another possible influence, and state a limit.
learning_goals: Define basic brain and body system vocabulary.
Explain how nervous and endocrine systems influence behaviour.
Use fictional case evidence to support a biological explanation.
Make a decision and justify it with evidence.
teacher_notes: Start with neutral behaviours such as attention, reaction time, sleepiness, practice, hunger, or stress response.
When students overuse brain-region names, ask how the evidence connects to the claim.
When students ignore context, ask what else could affect the behaviour.
For extension, students compare a biological explanation with a learning or social explanation.
questions: What else could influence the behaviour?
What would better evidence look like?

timing.activity: Introduce nervous system, endocrine system, and behaviour.
Map brain/body systems to neutral behaviours.
Analyze fictional cases using evidence tables.

model.title: Biological explanation model
steps: Claim: the character's attention may be affected by sleep and stress response.
Evidence: the case mentions short sleep, distraction, and a timed test.
System: nervous and endocrine systems may both be involved.
Alternative: study habits, noise, motivation, or task difficulty may also matter.
Limit: the case does not prove a medical or psychological diagnosis.

bullets: Brain and body systems influence behaviour.

content.scenario: A fictional student has trouble focusing after poor sleep and a stressful morning.

content.left: Weak: It is just their brain.
content.right: Stronger: brain/body systems, sleep, context, practice, and task demands may all matter.

rows: Do not diagnose from class examples.

task_sheet.title: A2 Student Packet — Brain, Body, and Behaviour
task_sheet.purpose_line: Use fictional case evidence to connect biological systems to behaviour carefully.
instructions: Make a decision and give a reason.
Include another possible influence and a limit.

tasks.label: Vocabulary organizer
tasks.prompt: Define neuron, neurotransmitter, nervous system, endocrine system, hormone, brain region, stress response, context, limit.

tasks.label: Supported pathway
tasks.prompt: Use the system map to match evidence to nervous system, endocrine system, behaviour, and context.

tasks.label: Proficient pathway
tasks.prompt: Choose the strongest biological explanation for the fictional case. Give evidence and a limit.

tasks.label: Extending pathway
tasks.prompt: Compare a biological explanation with a contextual explanation and decide which is better supported.

tasks.label: Final assessment
tasks.prompt: Write a careful brain/body case explanation using system, evidence, context, alternative, and limit.

success_criteria: I gave a decision plus reason.

exit_ticket.title: A2 Exit Ticket
exit_ticket.prompt: What is one brain/body explanation you can support from evidence, and what is one limit?
